label: Narrow-shell 3BR / baths CLUSTERED with SIDE CORRIDOR and full wet strip — the GENEROUS sibling of the side_corridor family: kitchen, common T&B and ensuite form one contiguous east strip; master owns the full rear band as a retreat opening to a rear lanai. Open great_room + kitchen (gr style). Tuned for 7.0 x 14.0-15.0 m shells.

adjacencies.note: kitchen heads the east strip behind the great_room, open over a counter (gr style) — at generous widths it earns a breakfast ledge
corridor mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
br2 doors east into the corridor
br3 doors east into the corridor
standards stack front-to-rear in the west column — wall only
master's door terminates the corridor — the full rear band is the retreat
common doors west into the corridor, midway between the standards and the great_room
kitchen → common on the east strip, first shared plumbing wall
common → ensuite continues the strip: kitchen + common + ensuite form ONE contiguous wet run — the clustered signature of this sibling
ensuite is the strip's rear cell, entered on its south wall from master's band

soft_proximities.note: keep the shared bath across the corridor from the rear standard

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_nw_side_corridor_baths_cl_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: Generous-first topology: the profile triggers already at 95 m² because the three-cell strip (kitchen + common + ensuite) wants ~7.5 m of depth on its own. Ensuite entering from the master band costs the strip an extra interior door offset — below ~88 m² that geometry stops paying and the ds sibling takes over (fallback), keeping the family solvable down the size ladder.

notes: 3BR narrow catalog topology #4 — SIDE CORRIDOR, CLUSTERED strip: the
GENEROUS sibling of #2. Natural floor band ~86-100 m²; designed against the
generous test: 11.0 x 19.0 m lot (2.0 m setbacks) → 7.0 x 15.0 m shell
(105 m²); happiest at 7.0 x 14.0-15.0. Regime: single-detached, no firewalls.
Layout intent (west = left, front = bottom):
Rear:  [master (full width, rear lanai)        ]
..     [br3     | HALL | ensuite               ]
..     [br2     | HALL | common T&B            ]
..     [        |      | kitchen               ]
Front: [great_room (entry, full width)         ]
Program: same three-sub-column parti as #2 but the east strip runs the full
depth as one wet bay — kitchen, common T&B, then ensuite, each sharing a
plumbing wall (single stack + vent for the entire house, the cheapest wet
layout of all twelve 3BR topologies). Master owns the full rear band and
opens through the rear wall to a lanai in the 2.0 m rear setback — the
deep-lot reward move. At 7.0 m width the west column holds 3.3 m bedrooms,
the corridor 1.0 m, the strip 2.2 m.
Ventilation: west column vents west, strip vents east, master vents rear +
both sides. The generous depth also buys a linen/storage run along the
corridor at 14 m+ shells.
Ensuite access: entered from master's band on the strip's south face — the
strip's rear cell belongs to the master suite even though it plumbs with
the service strip.
Downscale behavior: below ~88 m² the three-cell strip stops fitting —
fallback to #2 (ds sibling), which drops the ensuite out of the strip into
master's alcove. Chain continues #2 → split_columns for compact shells.
Carport policy (deferred): one side setback 2.0 → 3.0 m later (lot +1.0 m);
mirror so the strip faces the carport for the shortest service path.
